questions.q: The line passing through the extremity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> of the major axis and extremity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> of the minor axis of the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>9</mn><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>9</mn></math> meets its auxiliary circle at the point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math>. Then, the area of the triangle with vertices at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>M</mi></math> and the origin <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>O</mi></math> is

x2+y2=9   ..i

∴ Equation of AM is x3+y1=1   ...ii

On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

M-125,95

Now, area of ΔAOM=12OA×MN=2710 sq units

Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: $\mathrm{S}$ and $\mathrm{T}$ are the foci of an ellipse and $\mathrm{B}$ is an end of the minor axis. If STB is an equilateral triangle, then the eccentricity of the ellipse is
questions.solution:
Let eq. of ellipse be $\frac{x^{2}}{a^{2}}+\frac{y^{2}}{b^{2}}=1$, $\mathrm{S}$ is $(-\mathrm{ae}, 0), \mathrm{T}$ is $(\mathrm{ae}, 0)$ and $\mathrm{B}$ is $(0, \mathrm{~b})$

$\Rightarrow \mathrm{SB}=\sqrt{(0+\mathrm{ae})^{2}+\mathrm{b}^{2}}$
Also $\mathrm{SB}^{2}=\mathrm{ST}^{2} \Rightarrow 4 \mathrm{a}^{2} \mathrm{e}^{2}=\mathrm{a}^{2} \mathrm{e}^{2}+\mathrm{b}^{2}$
$\Rightarrow 3 a^{2} e^{2}=a^{2}\left(1-e^{2}\right)=a^{2}-a^{2} e^{2}$
$\Rightarrow 4 a^{2} e^{2}=a^{2} \Rightarrow e^{2}=\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow e=\frac{1}{2}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The equation of the ellipse whose centre is at the origin and the $X$-axis is major axis, which passes through the points $(-3,1)$ and $(2,-2)$ is
questions.solution:
Let equation of ellipse be $\frac{x^{2}}{a^{2}}+\frac{y^{2}}{b^{2}}=1$
Since it passes through the points $(-3,1)$ and $(2-2)$
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\therefore \quad \frac{(-3)^{2}}{a^{2}}+\frac{(1)^{2}}{b^{2}}=1 \text { and } \frac{(2)^{2}}{a^{2}}+\frac{(-2)^{2}}{b^{2}}=1 \\
&\Rightarrow \quad \frac{9}{a^{2}}+\frac{1}{b^{2}}=1 \quad \text{...(i)} \\
&\Rightarrow \quad \frac{4}{a^{2}}+\frac{4}{b^{2}}=1 \quad \text{...(ii)}
\end{aligned}
$$
Multiply by 4 in Eq. (i) and subtracting Eq. (i) from Eq. (ii),
$$
3 a^{2}=32 \Rightarrow a^{2}=\frac{32}{3}
$$
Substituting in Eq. (i), gives
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{9 \times 3}{32}+\frac{1}{b^{2}}=1 \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{1}{b^{2}}=1-\frac{27}{32}=\frac{5}{32} \Rightarrow b^{2}=\frac{32}{5}
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Required equation is
$$
\frac{3 x^{2}}{32}+\frac{5 y^{2}}{32}=1 \text { or } 3 x^{2}+5 y^{2}=32
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: If the length of the major axis of the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is three times the length of minor axis, its eccentricity is
questions.solution:
We have

x2a2+y2b2=1

Given,

Length of the major axis=3Length of the minor axis

⇒2a=32b

⇒a=3b

Now,

e=1-b2a2

⇒e=1-b29b2

⇒ e=1-19=83=223

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the distance between the foci of an ellipse is equal to the length of the latusrectum, then its eccentricity is
questions.solution:
Given,
In ellipse the distance between the foci
= Length of the latusrectum 
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow \quad & \text { 2ae }=\frac{2 b^{2}}{a} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & \quad a^{2} e=b^{2} \Rightarrow e=\frac{b^{2}}{a^{2}}\end{array}$
$\because \quad e^{2}=1-\frac{b^{2}}{a^{2}}$
$\Rightarrow \quad e^{2}=1-e \quad$ [from Eq.
$\Rightarrow e^{2}+e-1=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad e=\frac{-1 \pm \sqrt{1+4}}{2}$
(by quadratic formula) $\Rightarrow \quad e=\frac{-1 \pm \sqrt{5}}{2}$
$\Rightarrow$
$$
e=\frac{\sqrt{5}-1}{2} \quad(\because 1>e>0)
$$

questions.q: A man running a race-course notes that the sum of the distances from the two flag posts from him is always $10 \mathrm{~m}$ and the distance between the flag posts is $8 \mathrm{~m}$. The equations of the path traced by the man is given by
questions.solution:
Given that, $S P+S^{\prime} P=10$
$\Rightarrow \quad 2 a=10 \Rightarrow a=5$
and $\quad 2 a e=8$
$a e=4$
$b^{2}=a^{2}\left(1-e^{2}\right)$
$=a^{2}-(a e)^{2}$
$=25-16=9$
Hence, required equation is
$$
\frac{x^{2}}{25}+\frac{y^{2}}{9}=1
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Given the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>E</mi></mfenced><mo> </mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>9</mn><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>36</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>C</mi></mfenced><mo> </mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and two points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>,</mo></math> which of the following is correct?
questions.solution:
Given the ellipse E≡4x2+9y2-36=0......................i

 the circle C≡x2+y2-9=0........................ii

and two points Ax1,y1≡(1, 2), Bx2,y2≡(2, 1)

Now, E1≡4x12+9y12-36=412+922-36=4>0

So A lies outside E.

E2≡4x22+9y22-36=422+912-36=-11<0

So B lies inside E.

C1≡x12+y12-9=12+22-9=-4<0

So A lies inside C.

C2≡x22+y22-9=22+12-9=-4<0

So B lies inside C.

From the given options, option 4 is correct.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The ellipse having its foci $(0, \pm 1)$ and major axis of length $\sqrt{5}$ is
questions.solution:
Since the foci of given ellipse lie on the $Y$-axis, it is vertical ellipse.
Let the required equation be $\frac{x^2}{b^2}+\frac{y^2}{a^2}=1$
Where $a^2>b^2$
Foci are $(0, \pm c)=(0, \pm 1) \Rightarrow c=1$
$a=$ length of the semi-major axis $=\frac{1}{2} \times \sqrt{5}=\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}$
$\therefore \quad a^2=\frac{5}{4}$
Now, $\quad c^2=a^2-b^2 \Rightarrow 1=\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}\right)^2-b^2$
$\Rightarrow \quad b^2=\frac{5}{4}-1=\frac{1}{4}$
$\therefore$ The required equation is
$\frac{x^2}{1 / 4}+\frac{y^2}{5 / 4}=1 \Rightarrow 4 x^2+\frac{4 y^2}{5}=1$
$\Rightarrow \quad 20 x^2+4 y^2=5$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: For some <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> if the eccentricity of the hyperbola, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>sec</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt></math> times the eccentricity of the ellipse, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>sec</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the length of the latus rectum of the ellipse, is
questions.solution:
For hyperbola x210-y210cos2θ=1

The eccentricity of the hyperbola eH=1+b2a2=1+cos2θ 

For ellipse x25cos2θ+y25=1

The eccentricity of an ellipse eE=1-b2a2=1-cos2θ=sinθ

Given, eH=5 eE

⇒1+cos2θ=5sin2θ⇒cos2θ=23 

Now, the length of the latus rectum of an ellipse =2b2a=10cos2θ5=2035=453
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: An ellipse has foci <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mn>4,2</mn></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mfenced separators="|"><mn>2,2</mn></mfenced></math> and it passes through the point <math><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2,4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>. The eccentricity of the ellipse is
questions.solution:
Let, 4,2=S1 and 2,2=S2 and eccentricity of ellipse is e
then S1S2=2ae and PS1+PS2=2a
(where 2a is length of major axis)
⇒e=S1S2PS1+PS2=222+2
⇒e=12+1=2-1=tanπ8
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{" separators="|"><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>∈</mo><msup><mi>R</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>:</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>r</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>-</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>r</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> where <math><mi>r</mi><mo>≠</mo><mo>±</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math> Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> represents:
options: An ellipse whose eccentricity is <math><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mi>r</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> when <math><mi>r</mi><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math>

A hyperbola whose eccentricity is <math><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mi>r</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>r</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math>

An ellipse whose eccentricity is  <math><msqrt><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></msqrt><mo>,</mo></math>  when  <math><mi>r</mi><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn></math>

A hyperbola whose eccentricity is  <math><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>r</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math>  when  <math><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>r</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn></math>
questions.solution:
Given y21+r-x21-r=1

If r∈0,1 then this curve is a hyperbola whose eccentricity is 1+1-r1+r=2r+1. 

If r>1 , then this curve is an ellipse whose
eccentricity is 1-r-1r+1=2r+1.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> be the ellipse <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>16</mn></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>9</mn></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>. For any three distinct points <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>Q</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Q</mi><mo>'</mo></msup></math> on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math>, let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>P</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>Q</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> be the mid-point of the line segment joining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi></math>, and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>P</mi><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Q</mi><mo>'</mo></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> be the mid-point of the line segment joining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Q</mi><mo>'</mo></msup></math>. Then the maximum possible value of the distance between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>P</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>Q</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>P</mi><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>Q</mi><mo>'</mo></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>, as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>Q</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Q</mi><mo>'</mo></msup></math> vary on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math>, is _____.
questions.solution:
.

In a triangle sides joining the mid-points of two sides is half and parallel to third side

M1M2=12QQ'

Maximum value of QQ' is AA'

Hence, maximum value of M1M2=12AA'=4
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: An ellipse is drawn by taking a diameter of the circle $(x-1)^2+y^2=1$ as its semiminor axis and a diameter of the circle $x^2+(y-2)^2=4$ as its semi-major axis. If the centre of the ellipse is the origin and its axes are the coordinate axes, then the equation of the ellipse is
questions.solution:
Semi minor axis $b=2$
Semi major axis $a=4$
Equation of ellipse $=\frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \quad \Rightarrow \frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{y^2}{4}=1$
$\Rightarrow x^2+4 y^2=16$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q:

B=a, b∈R×R:4a-62+9b-52≤36. Then :

Given sets, A=a, b∈R×R :a-5<1 and b-5<1 

A=a, b∈R×R :-1<a-5<1 and -1<b-5<1 

A=a, b∈R×R :4<a<6 and 4<b<6 

B=a-62 9+b-524≤1 

For set A, 

For corner coordinates of the set A is 4,4,4,6,6,4,6,3. 

For set B, 

Now, Put 4,4 in the equation of the set B, 

49+14≤1, 4,4 lies in the set B. 

Similarly, 4,6,6,4,6,3 also lies in the set B. 

So, A⊂B .
